literal_meaning: the five heaps are indeed a burden
preferred_translation: the five heaps are indeed a burden
alternative_translations: the five aggregates are a burden
definition: Canonical burden formula applied to the five heaps.
notes: Use this phrase record when the burden imagery is foregrounded. It inherits the house default for pañcakkhandhā and keeps the family distinct from the appropriated expression pañcupādānakkhandhā unless the source text explicitly uses the latter instead.

context_rules.context: In direct doctrinal translation of the burden formula
context_rules.rendering: the five heaps are indeed a burden

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose using familiar aggregate terminology
context_rules.rendering: the five aggregates are a burden
context_rules.notes: Use this as a controlled alternate for orientation.

example_phrases.translation: the five heaps are indeed a burden
example_phrases.notes: Canonical burden line for the family.
